key_insight: SLiR turns many activation functions into optimizable linear bounds by choosing a slope and then shifting the line to guarantee sound upper and lower relaxations.
engineering_relevance: This makes neural network verification easier to extend to new activations, reducing manual relaxation design while preserving correctness guarantees.
method: Compute the intercept via a shifting procedure that enforces sound bounds over the domain.
Use only a Lipschitz constant or critical points, making the method broadly applicable.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports tight relaxations across many practical activation functions and verification of up to 7.8x more properties than state-of-the-art methods.
watch_item: Soundness depends on correct slope/shift construction and suitable domain information, so implementation errors or weak bounds could reduce verification benefit.
paper_title: Shifting-based Optimizable Linear Relaxations for General Activation Functions
